prompt: Write a 150-word short story about a lighthouse keeper who discovers something unexpected in the fog. Do not use dialogue.
response: In the thick of fog, lighthouse keeper Edmund diligently tended the light, his routine unchanged for decades. One night, a peculiar scent wafted in with the mist—sweet, exotic, unlike the usual salt and seaweed. Intrigued, he stepped onto the walkway, squinting into the haze. A soft rustling echoed below, then a splash. He hurried down the spiral stairs, heart pounding. At the shore, he found it: a small boat, vividly painted, bearing crates of strange fruits. A parrot squawked from its perch on the prow. Edmund was awestruck. He'd heard tales of distant lands, but never had they come to him. As dawn broke, the fog lifted, revealing no sign of the boat. Only a ripe mango nestled in the sand, proof of the night's strange visit.
